MonoCloud for Startups

# MonoCloud for Startups

Launching today

## One identity layer for your customers, APIs, and agents

17 followers

## One identity layer for your customers, APIs, and agents

17 followers

Visit website

Authentication & identity tools

MonoCloud is one identity layer for your customers, your APIs, and your agents. Most tools stop at a login box. We go past login into authorization and accountability: decide exactly what every user, service, and AI agent can access, prove what it did, and revoke it in an instant. Fine-grained Cedar authorization, passkeys and SSO, API protection, M2M, and mTLS with certificate-bound trust, all on one platform. Startups get the full platform free for one year. [...] We built MonoCloud so you do not do that. It is one identity layer for your customers, your APIs, and your agents. Login is table stakes. The part that matters is what happens after: you decide exactly what every user, service, and agent can access, prove what it did, and revoke it in an instant, before an agent is ever issued a token.

Most tools make you pick a side. The login-box platforms stop at the sign-in screen. The enterprise suites are powerful but heavy and built for large companies only. MonoCloud is the one platform that covers customer identity, API protection, and agent identity together, from your very first user all the way to enterprise scale.

What you can do with it: [...] MonoCloud covers all of it on one platform, your customers, your APIs, and your agents, from your first user to enterprise scale. Not just who signs in, but what every user, service, and agent is allowed to do afterwards, with the ability to prove it and pull it back in an instant.

All of it is live today, not a demo or a waitlist. Ten design partners across SaaS and AI companies are already building on it.

👉 If you are a startup, you get the full platform free for a year, every feature, no gates. Apply here: MonoCloud for Startups - Free for One Year 👈

And if identity is central to what you are building and you want to shape where this goes, we are opening a few more design partner spots. Leave a comment or reach me on LinkedIn (link in profile).

# MonoCloud for StartupsAlternatives

Comparing MonoCloud for Startups? Explore identity stacks like Clerk for polished UIs, Auth0 for enterprise-grade SSO and rules, Descope for drag-and-drop flows, PropelAuth for fast, code-light setup, and WorkOS for SAML/SCIM and audits—great for scaling teams and B2B needs.

results.title: When did you actually start taking auth seriously?
results.content:
Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

@harri\_cuevas the triggers vary but the most common ones i see are: first enterprise customer asking for SSO, first security questionnaire, or first near-miss with a credential. user count rarely drives it, risk does.

and if you're building with AI agents, it's a no-brainer now. the PocketOS incident is a good example. an agent deleted their entire production database in under ten seconds because it found an over-scoped token and used it exactly as allowed. no break-in, just an agent doing what it was permitted to do with credentials nobody thought to scope properly.|

agents need their own identity, not borrowed credentials. that's exactly one of the interesting parts we're building at MonoCloud :)

Report [...] Report

Bryan Williamson

@scarlett\_massey security is one of those areas where everything feels fine until suddenly it isn't. Spending time on auth before there is a problem can feel unnecessary, but fixing it under pressure is usually much harder.

Report

Simran Kumar

@scarlett\_massey  @bryan\_williamson3 One thing I've learned is that authentication is only one part of the picture. Permissions, roles session , recovery flows, and audit logs often become the bigger challenges as the product grows.

Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups [...] MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

@simran\_kumar yes! this is the part most people don't see coming. login is the easy bit. it's the permissions, session management, and audit trails that quietly become the real problem as the product grows. that's actually what we built MonoCloud around.

Report

Malani Willa

@scarlett\_massey  @bryan\_williamson3 the first customer asking "how do you handle security?" is probably the moment many founders realize they need to move beyond the quick prototype setup. It changes the conversation from building features to building trust.

Report

Riya Pariyar

MonoCloud for Startups

MonoCloud for Startups

Right now, we are all forcing our agents to live as "ghosts" in the machine borrowing our emails, hacking our 2FA, and piggybacking on our identities. This doesn't scale, and it isn't secure.

We built Loomal.ai to be the foundational identity layer for the Agentic Age.

### What does "Agent-Native Infrastructure" actually look like?

Loomal lets you charge for what you sell online — API calls, tools, digital products, or your whole store. One line of code (or a Shopify/WooCommerce plugin) adds an agent-ready paywall: AI agents pay you in USDC, settled in about 2 seconds, and you keep 100% of your revenue — no percentage cut, ever. Free to start, no card; flat monthly plans as you grow. Every paid listing appears on the Loomal Index, where agents discover and pay. Launch offer: first 500 sellers get 1,000 transactions free.
